subtopicMappings.description: Explicitly marked as Not Applicable to A Level Further Mathematics
Explicitly marked as Not Applicable to A Level Further Mathematics
No canonical node in the supplied catalog covers consistency/infinite versus no-solution classification for linear simultaneous equations.
No canonical node for the discrete uniform distribution in the supplied catalog.
No canonical node for problem-type classification (existence, construction, enumeration, optimisation) exists in the supplied catalog.
No canonical node for the pigeonhole principle exists in the supplied catalog.
No canonical node for derangements exists in the supplied catalog.
no group theory nodes in catalog
No canonical node for Fibonacci numbers, Lucas numbers, or the golden ratio.
No canonical node for first-order linear recurrence relations with constant coefficients.
No canonical node for second-order linear recurrence relations with constant coefficients.
No canonical node for modelling with recurrence relations.
No canonical node for second-order recurrence modelling.
No canonical node for number bases or positional notation with arbitrary base.
No canonical node for the division algorithm or quotient-remainder representation.
No canonical node for modular arithmetic or finite arithmetics.
No canonical node for linear congruences.
No canonical node for quadratic residues.
No canonical node for simultaneous linear congruences or the Chinese remainder theorem.
No canonical node for Euclid's lemma.
No canonical node for Fermat's little theorem or pseudoprimes.
No canonical node for the order of an element modulo a prime.
No canonical node for the order of an element modulo a prime dividing p-1.
No canonical node for binary operations or their properties on abstract sets.
No canonical node for Cayley tables.
No canonical node for the axiomatic definition of a group.
No canonical node for the Latin square property of group tables.
No canonical node for the order of a group or order of an element.
No canonical node for cyclic groups.
No canonical node for generators of cyclic or finitely generated groups.
No canonical node for the structure of finite groups up to order seven.
No canonical node for groups of higher finite order or infinite order.
No canonical node for Lagrange's theorem.
No canonical node for group isomorphism.
No canonical node for groups defined by algebraic properties or abstract algebraic proof.
No canonical node for functions of two variables or 3-D surfaces.
No canonical node for functions of more than two variables.
No canonical node for sections and contours of 3-D surfaces.
No canonical node for partial differentiation or mixed partial derivatives.
No canonical node for stationary points of functions of two variables.
No canonical node for the Hessian matrix or classifying stationary points of 3-D surfaces.
No canonical node for tangent planes to 3-D surfaces.
Broad cross-domain notation requirement spanning number systems, algebra, calculus, geometry, probability, statistics and mechanics; no single canonical node mapping is appropriate.
Broad recall/use of formulae and identities across pure mathematics, mechanics and statistics; individual items are not mapped to single canonical nodes in this extraction.

List of formulae provided in assessments spans pure mathematics, matrices, calculus, numerical methods, complex numbers, vectors, trigonometry, hyperbolic functions, simple harmonic motion and statistics; no single node mapping.

subtopicMappings.subtopicName: OCR H245 specification, p.122, Inclusion-exclusion principle
subtopicMappings.description: No canonical node for the inclusion-exclusion counting principle in the supplied catalog.

subtopicMappings.subtopicName: OCR H245 specification, p.122, The hierarchy of orders
subtopicMappings.description: No canonical node for computational complexity order hierarchy (Big O notation) in the supplied catalog.
No canonical node covers Hessian matrix or classification of stationary points of 3-D surfaces z = f(x,y).
No canonical node covers the equation of a tangent plane to a 3-D surface using partial derivatives.
